body.title: [triage] The intent extractor's LIVE success rate is 0% — and the two causes are now NAMED: minion returns a JSON ARRAY (few-shot read as a batch), worker returns EMPTY (reasoning model truncated at max_tokens=256, finish_reason='length' unread)
body.intention: I intend that the intent extractor actually PRODUCES intents — and that when it cannot, the record distinguishes a model that answered wrongly from a model that was never allowed to finish answering. A response truncated mid-reasoning is a BUDGET failure, not a PARSE failure, and filing one as the other is the same two-causes-one-signal collapse this subsystem was just fixed for. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**MEASURED LIVE 2026-07-13** against a clean, unloaded Hex (see
T-default-suite-drives-live-inference-and-saturates-hex — the box had been saturated by our own
orphaned test runs; that is fixed and NOT the cause here).

This is **criterion 3** of the just-closed `T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal`, finally
measurable. **Result: 0 / 5. A 0% success rate.** All five live predictions were recorded
`provenance_class='error'` — correctly, honestly, and *legibly*, which is exactly what that ticket
bought. **That ticket fixed VISIBILITY, not FUNCTION — and that was the right scope. This ticket is
the function, and it is only diagnosable BECAUSE of the visibility fix.** Before today this was five
identical `unknown`s wearing CP1's clothes. Today the store says:

    [5x] parse: not JSON (Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)) [tier=worker]

**BOTH CAUSES ARE NOW CAPTURED RAW. They are different, and the escalation ladder runs from one
straight into the other.**

**CAUSE 1 — minion (`llama3.2:3b`) reads the few-shot block as a BATCH and returns a JSON ARRAY.**
Handed the exact prompt `predict()` builds (~1,938 tokens, 10 examples), it classifies **all eleven
contexts** and emits:

    Here are the intents classified as JSON:
    [ {"intent": "Error handling", "confidence": 0.9},
      {"intent": "Test reliability", "confidence": 0.8},
      ... one entry per few-shot example ...
      {"intent": "Escalation policy refinement", "confidence": 0.82} ]

**This is the original `parsed.get()`-on-a-list crash, reproduced live for the first time.**
`device.py`'s own comment predicted it verbatim ("a minion-tier model handed ten long few-shot
examples and asked for structured output returns a JSON *array*") — now it is evidence, not a guess.
The real defect is the FORMAT: we stuff few-shot examples into a **single user message**, which is
completion-style few-shot fed to a **chat** API. The chat-native form is alternating
`user`/`assistant` message pairs, where an example is structurally an *exchange*, not text the model
must guess is illustrative. With a short prompt and no few-shot block, the SAME model returns clean,
valid JSON — proving the model is fine and the prompt shape is not.

**CAUSE 2 — worker (`deepseek-r1:14b`) is a REASONING model and never gets to answer.**
Same prompt, escalated tier:

    text = ''
    finish_reason = 'length'      <-- THE SIGNAL WAS ALWAYS THERE

`max_tokens=256` is consumed *entirely* by its `<think>` trace; it is cut off before emitting a single
token of answer. Empty string -> `json.loads("")` -> `Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)`.

**THE THIRD DEFECT, WHICH IS THE DAY'S PATTERN ONE MORE TIME: `finish_reason` IS NEVER READ.** A
model that was TRUNCATED and a model that emitted PROSE are two entirely different failures with two
entirely different fixes — and we file both as `parse: not JSON`. **Two causes, one signal.** The
shape-injectivity work stopped a crash from impersonating a refusal; it did not stop a *budget*
failure from impersonating a *parse* failure. The map is still not injective, one level in.

**Scope boundary:** IN — (a) few-shot examples become real chat-native `user`/`assistant` message
pairs, not a text blob in one user turn; (b) `finish_reason` is read, and `length` is recorded as its
own cause (`truncated: hit max_tokens at tier=X`), never as a parse failure; (c) the token budget fits
the routed model — a reasoning model needs headroom for its trace, or must not be routed to a
structured-output task class at all. OUT — the crash-vs-refusal record (done); Hex capacity (separate
ticket); model selection policy generally.

**Test plan / discriminating fixture:** feed a stub source that returns (1) a JSON array, (2) `''`
with `finish_reason='length'`, (3) valid JSON. The three must land as **three distinct records** —
`parse`, `truncated`, and `model`. A build that collapses (1) and (2) into one `parse` error fails
this fixture, which is the entire point. Plus a live check: `predict()` against Hex must produce a
`provenance_class='model'` row.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. A truncated response and an unparseable response are stored as DIFFERENT causes.
  2. Live success rate on a clean Hex is measured again and is materially > 0 (state the number).
  3. The few-shot corpus is delivered as chat message pairs; the array-batch output does not recur.
  4. Whatever remains failing is NAMED — no silent 'unknown'.
